post.summary: When a row is added, modified or deleted
post.description: This trigger allows you to start a flow when a row is added, modified or deleted in a Microsoft Dataverse table, such that it matches the selected options. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: Consistency
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse by pass the cache and hit DB

parameters.name: catalog
parameters.description: Using this header parameter to save catalog in flow definition and Microsoft Dataverse will ignore as they don't use this info

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Using this header parameter to save category in flow definition and Microsoft Dataverse will ignore as they don't use this info

post.summary: When a row is added, modified, or deleted in selected environment
post.description: This trigger allows you to start a flow when a row is added, modified or deleted in a Microsoft Dataverse table in the current or selected Power Platform environment.

parameters.name: Consistency
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse by pass the cache and hit DB

parameters.name: catalog
parameters.description: Using this header parameter to save catalog in flow definition and Microsoft Dataverse will ignore as they don't use this info

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Using this header parameter to save category in flow definition and Microsoft Dataverse will ignore as they don't use this info

get.description: This action allows you to list the rows in a Microsoft Dataverse table that match the selected options. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: prefer
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the created resource

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the odata id

parameters.name: x-ms-odata-metadata-full
parameters.description: The header parameter to customer to opt-in returning full odata metadata in response.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Name of the target Dataverse table (e.g., 'contacts', 'access_rights'). Use get_tables to discover valid names.

parameters.name: $select
parameters.description: Enter a comma-separated list of column unique names to limit which columns are listed
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. Comma-separated list of column names to return. If omitted, all columns are returned. Example: "id,email,status".

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.description: Enter an OData style filter expression to limit which rows are listed
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: OData filter string. Supported operators: eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, and, or. Wrap all string/date values in single quotes. No functions (contains, startswith, etc.) are supported. Example: "status eq 'Active' and system eq 'CoreBanking'".

parameters.name: $orderby
parameters.description: Columns to sort by in OData orderBy style (excluding lookups)
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. Sort order as "column_name direction". Direction is 'asc' or 'desc' (default: asc). Example: "granted_at desc".

parameters.name: $expand
parameters.description: Enter an Odata style expand query to list related rows

parameters.name: fetchXml
parameters.description: Enter a Fetch XML query for advanced customization

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Enter the number of rows to be listed (default = 5000)
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. Maximum number of rows to return. Range: 1-1000, default: 1000. Set to a smaller value when you only need a few results.

parameters.name: $skiptoken
parameters.description: Enter the skip token obtained from a previous run to list rows from the next page

parameters.name: partitionId
parameters.description: An option to specify the partitionId while retrieving data for NoSQL tables

get.x-ms-description-for-model: Lists rows from a Dataverse table with optional OData filtering, sorting, pagination, and field selection. The $filter parameter supports ONLY: comparison operators (eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le) and boolean operators (and, or). String values in filters must be wrapped in single quotes: "status eq 'Active'". NOT supported: contains(), startswith(), endswith(), or any OData functions. NOT supported: bare date literals -- wrap dates in single quotes like strings: "created_date ge '2024-01-01'". Use get_tables to discover valid table names.

post.description: This action allows you to add a new row in the selected Microsoft Dataverse table. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: prefer
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the created resource

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the odata id

parameters.name: x-ms-odata-metadata-full
parameters.description: The header parameter to customer to opt-in returning full odata metadata in response.
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. If true, returns full OData metadata in the response. Defaults to false. Usually omit this parameter.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Name of the target Dataverse table (e.g., 'contacts', 'access_rights'). Use get_tables to discover valid names.

parameters.name: item
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: REQUIRED. A JSON object containing ALL field values for the new row. Every column value must be inside this object -- do not pass them as top-level parameters. Keys must match the column names in the table schema exactly (case-sensitive).

post.x-ms-description-for-model: Creates a new row in a Dataverse table. Requires exactly two parameters: 'entityName' (table name) and 'item' (a JSON object containing ALL row data). Every field for the new row MUST go inside the 'item' object -- do NOT pass row fields as top-level parameters. Example call: {"entityName": "access_rights", "item": {"employee_id": "EMP-001", "status": "Active"}}. Returns the created row. Use get_tables to discover valid table names. The optional 'x-ms-odata-metadata-full' parameter can usually be omitted (defaults to false).

get.description: List rows from a table in a Power Platform environment.
get.x-ms-description-for-model: Use tool to search, list, or get rows or records from a Dataverse table in a specific Power Platform environment. Example user intent: List all open cases in xyz table from abc environment, Find contacts named Alex Smith.

parameters.name: prefer
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the created resource

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the odata id

parameters.name: x-ms-odata-metadata-full
parameters.description: The header parameter to customer to opt-in returning full odata metadata in response.
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to true only when full OData metadata for each record is needed. Otherwise leave false. Must be ONLY true or false.

parameters.name: organization
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to the name or ID of the Power Platform environment containing the Dataverse table. Example: Default or Prod-CRM-Europe.

parameters.name: MSCRM.IncludeMipSensitivityLabel
parameters.description: Select if you want to fetch Sensitivity Label ID
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to true when sensitivity label ID for records is required. Otherwise leave false. Must be ONLY true or false.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to the logical name of the Dataverse table to query. Examples: accounts, contacts, incidents.

parameters.name: $select
parameters.description: Enter a comma-separated list of column unique names to limit which columns are listed
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set only when user asks for specific fields. Set to a comma-separated string of column logical names, not display labels. Leave empty to return all columns. Example: name,emailaddress1,telephone1.

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.description: Enter an OData style filter expression to limit which rows are listed
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to an OData $filter string using column logical names, operators eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, logical and or or, and functions contains, startswith, endswith. Wrap string values in single quotes. No SQL or natural language. Example: statuscode eq 1 and contains(fullname, 'Smith').

parameters.name: $orderby
parameters.description: Columns to sort by in OData orderBy style (excluding lookups)
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to one or more column logical names followed by asc or desc to control sort order. Examples: createdon desc, fullname asc.

parameters.name: $expand
parameters.description: Enter an Odata style expand query to list related rows
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to an OData $expand expression when related records from lookup or relationship columns are needed. Example: primarycontactid($select=fullname,emailaddress1).

parameters.name: fetchXml
parameters.description: Enter a Fetch XML query for advanced customization
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to the full FetchXML query string including <fetch>...</fetch> XML when requested by user or configuration. Example: <fetch version="1.0" output-format="xml-platform" mapping="logical">...</fetch>.

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Enter rows to list. The default is 2000. Enable paging in settings for more rows.
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to maximum number of rows to return. Must be positive integer. Example: 10, 100.

parameters.name: $skiptoken
parameters.description: Enter the skip token obtained from a previous run to list rows from the next page
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to the $skiptoken string from a previous List rows call to get the next page. Only use if previous response included a skip token and more rows are wanted. Never invent this value.

parameters.name: partitionId
parameters.description: An option to specify the partitionId while retrieving data for NoSQL tables
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to the partition ID string for a NoSQL table using the value from configuration or instructions. Example: sales-partition-01.

x-ms-content-sensitivitylabel-id.description: Sensitivity Label id for item content.

@odata.nextLink.description: The url to fetch next page data.

post.summary: Add a new row to selected environment
post.description: Create a new row in a table in a Power Platform environment.
post.x-ms-description-for-model: Use this tool to insert or create a row in a Dataverse table in a specific Power Platform environment. Use for intents such as creating contacts, accounts, leads, opportunities, incidents, or other entities. Example requests: 'Create a new account in Dataverse', 'Add a ticket to the customer table'.

parameters.name: prefer
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the created resource

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the odata id

parameters.name: x-ms-odata-metadata-full
parameters.description: The header parameter to customer to opt-in returning full odata metadata in response.
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to true to include full OData metadata in response. False otherwise. If not specified default to false.

parameters.name: organization
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set this to the environment base URL containing the target Dataverse table. You MUST use a URL and NOT environment name. If name is provided, map to correct URL. If you cannot infer the base URL, default to URL of (Current). Examples: https://org12345.crm.dynamics.com/, https://exampleenv.crm.dynamics.com/

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set this to the entity set name for the Dataverse table in which you are adding the row. Examples: accounts, contacts, incidents, cr3a_customtables.

parameters.name: item
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set this to an object defining the new row. Keys must be column logical names (not display labels). For lookups, use @odata.bind format (e.g.,"PrimaryContactId@odata.bind": "/contacts(GUID)"). Example: {"name": "Contoso Ltd", "telephone1": "+1 425 555 0100"}.

get.description: This action allows you to get the row that matches an ID in a Microsoft Dataverse table. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: prefer
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the created resource

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the odata id

parameters.name: x-ms-odata-metadata-full
parameters.description: The header parameter to customer to opt-in returning full odata metadata in response.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Name of the target Dataverse table (e.g., 'contacts', 'access_rights'). Use get_tables to discover valid names.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Primary key value of the record. Provide exactly one of 'recordId' or 'filter', not both.

parameters.name: $select
parameters.description: Enter a comma-separated list of column unique names to limit which columns are listed
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. Comma-separated list of column names to return. If omitted, all columns are returned. Example: "id,email,status".

parameters.name: $expand
parameters.description: Enter an Odata style expand query to list related rows

parameters.name: partitionId
parameters.description: An option to specify the partitionId while retrieving data for NoSQL tables

get.x-ms-description-for-model: Retrieves a single row from a Dataverse table by primary key or field filter. Provide exactly one of 'recordId' (primary key lookup) or 'filter' (dict of field:value pairs) -- not both. Returns one row. For querying multiple rows, use list_rows instead.

delete.description: This action allows you to delete a row from a Microsoft Dataverse table. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Name of the target Dataverse table (e.g., 'accounts'). Use get_tables to discover valid names.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Primary key (GUID) of the row to delete. Must be a valid ID from the table.

parameters.name: partitionId
parameters.description: An option to specify the partitionId while deleting row for NoSQL tables
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. Partition key for NoSQL-backed tables. Omit for standard Dataverse tables.

delete.x-ms-description-for-model: Permanently deletes a row from a Dataverse table by its primary key (GUID). This action cannot be undone. Requires 'entityName' and 'recordId'. This removes the entire row -- to change specific fields without deleting, use update_row instead.

patch.description: This action allows you to modify any selected row in a Microsoft Dataverse table, or adds a new row if it doesn’t exist. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: prefer
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the created resource

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the odata id

parameters.name: x-ms-odata-metadata-full
parameters.description: The header parameter to customer to opt-in returning full odata metadata in response.
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. If true, returns full OData metadata in the response. Defaults to false. Usually omit this parameter.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Name of the target Dataverse table (e.g., 'contacts'). Use get_tables to discover valid names.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Primary key (GUID) of the row. If a row with this ID exists, it is updated; otherwise a new row is created.

parameters.name: item
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: REQUIRED. A JSON object containing the field values to set. All field values MUST be inside this object -- do not pass them as top-level parameters. Keys must match column names exactly.

patch.x-ms-description-for-model: Updates an existing row or inserts a new one if the recordId is not found (upsert). Requires: 'entityName', 'recordId' (the primary key GUID), and 'item' (a JSON object with field values). All field values MUST go inside the 'item' object -- do NOT pass them as top-level parameters. Example: {"entityName": "contacts", "recordId": "d5be32ad-...", "item": {"firstname": "Mary"}}. Unlike update_row, this does NOT fail if the record doesn't exist -- it creates a new row instead.

patch.description: This action allows you to modify any selected row in a Microsoft Dataverse table. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: prefer
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the created resource

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse return the odata id

parameters.name: x-ms-odata-metadata-full
parameters.description: The header parameter to customer to opt-in returning full odata metadata in response.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Name of the target Dataverse table (e.g., 'contacts', 'access_rights'). Use get_tables to discover valid names.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Primary key value of the record to update. Provide exactly one of 'recordId' or 'filter', not both. Value must match the table's primary key field.

parameters.name: item
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: REQUIRED. A JSON object of field-value pairs to update. All update values MUST be inside this object -- do not pass them as top-level parameters. Only include fields you want to change.

patch.x-ms-description-for-model: Updates one or more fields on an existing row in a Dataverse table. Requires: 'entityName', exactly one of 'recordId' or 'filter' to identify the target row, and 'item' containing the fields to change. All update values MUST go inside the 'item' object -- do NOT pass them as top-level parameters. Example: {"entityName": "access_rights", "recordId": "AR-001", "item": {"status": "Revoked"}}. Use 'recordId' when you have the primary key value; use 'filter' (a dict of field:value pairs) when matching on other fields. Provide exactly one of 'recordId' or 'filter', not both.

post.description: Predict

parameters.name: modelId
parameters.description: Choose an option or add your own

get.description: Gets operation schema for predict operation.

post.description: This action allows you to use the AI Builder models in your Microsoft Dataverse environment for predictions. Common models include Form Processing, Object Detection, Text Classification or use the prebuilt Business Card Reader, Key phrase extraction, Language detection, Text recognition – OCR and Sentiment Analysis.

parameters.name: Prefer
parameters.description: The header required to allow Dataverse to process the request async with 202 OK and location headers

post.summary: Add to feedback loop of AI Builder models (Preview)
post.description: [Preview] This action allows you to add feedback loop items to AI Builder models in your Microsoft Dataverse environment.

inputName.description: Name used for the prediction input

post.description: Use AI Builder models with a Microsoft Dataverse row Id. With the predict action you can use your custom AI Builder Models of Form Processing, Object Detection, Text Classification or use the prebuilt Business Card Reader, Key phrase extraction, Language detection, Text recognition – OCR and Sentiment Analysis.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Choose an option or add your own

post.description: This action allows you to link a row in one Microsoft Dataverse table to another if the tables have a one-to-many or many-to-many relationship. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Name of the table containing the primary row (e.g., 'contacts'). Use get_tables to discover valid names.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: GUID (primary key) of the primary row to create the relationship from. Must be a valid ID from the entityName table.

parameters.name: associationEntityRelationship
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Name of the Dataverse relationship to use (e.g., 'contact_customer_accounts'). Must be a valid relationship defined in the schema.

post.x-ms-description-for-model: Creates an association between two Dataverse rows via a named relationship. Supports one-to-many and many-to-many relationship types. The '@odata.id' parameter must be a URL pointing to the related row. The URL format is: https://<org>.crm.dynamics.com/api/data/v9.0/<entity_plural>(<guid>). The hostname, entity name, and GUID must match values from your Dataverse environment -- do not guess these.

delete.description: This action allows you to remove the link between a row in one Microsoft Dataverse table to another if the tables have a one-to-many or many-to-many relationship. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Name of the table containing the primary row (e.g., 'contacts'). Use get_tables to discover valid names.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: GUID (primary key) of the primary row to remove the relationship from. Must be a valid ID from the entityName table.

parameters.name: associationEntityRelationship
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Name of the Dataverse relationship to remove (e.g., 'contact_customer_accounts'). Must be a valid relationship defined in the schema.

parameters.name: $id
parameters.description: Enter the row URL using OData ID from a previous step or typing the full URL (eg. https://org0.crm.dynamics.com/api/data/v9.0/faxes(3ce6c728-3c8a-4b55-a4ee-a251b253c3ee)
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: OData URL of the related row to unrelate. Format: https://<org>.crm.dynamics.com/api/data/v9.0/<entity_plural>(<guid>). The hostname, entity name, and GUID must come from your environment data -- do not guess or use placeholder values.

delete.x-ms-description-for-model: Removes an existing association between two Dataverse rows. The inverse of relate_rows -- disconnects a relationship without deleting either row. The '$id' parameter must be a URL pointing to the related row. The URL format is: https://<org>.crm.dynamics.com/api/data/v9.0/<entity_plural>(<guid>). The hostname, entity name, and GUID must match values from your Dataverse environment -- do not guess these.

post.description: Associate individual rows in tables that have a one-to-many or many-to-many relationship in the same Power Platform environment.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)

delete.description: Remove the association between individual rows in a Power Platform environment.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)

parameters.name: $id
parameters.description: Enter the row URL using OData ID from a previous step or typing the full URL (eg. https://org0.crm.dynamics.com/api/data/v9.0/faxes(3ce6c728-3c8a-4b55-a4ee-a251b253c3ee)

This action allows you to perform Microsoft Dataverse actions available in the environment that are not associated with any table. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

post.summary: Perform an unbound action in selected environment
post.description: Run a global Dataverse action in a Power Platform environment, including custom actions.
post.x-ms-description-for-model: Use this tool to run, execute, or trigger an unbound Dataverse action in a Power Platform environment. Do not use for bound actions that target a specific record. Sample intents: 'Run the approval workflow in production'; 'Execute WhoAmI in Dataverse'.

parameters.name: organization
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to the URL of the target Power Platform environment where the action will run. Get this value from configuration or a prior tool call. Example: 'https://org1a2b3c.crm.dynamics.com'.

parameters.name: actionName
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to the name of the unbound Dataverse action to execute. Examples: 'WhoAmI', 'ExportSolution'.

parameters.name: item
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to a JSON object containing the input parameters required by the selected action. Include only the parameters defined for that action. Example { 'SolutionName': 'MySolution', 'Managed': true }.

post.description: This action allows you to perform Microsoft Dataverse actions associated with a selected table. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)

post.summary: Perform a bound action in selected environment
post.description: Run a Dataverse action bound to a table in a Power Platform environment, including custom actions.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)

get.description: Triggers a flow when a row is selected from the Flow menu in the ribbon.

post.description: This action allows you to perform a group of Microsoft Dataverse connector operations as a single transaction. If one of the operations fails, all the successful actions are rolled back. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

put.summary: Upload a file or an image
put.description: This action allows you to upload a file or an image content to a Microsoft Dataverse table with a compatible column type. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: content-type
parameters.description: The header describing the content type

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)

parameters.name: x-ms-file-name
parameters.description: Enter a name for the file or image

put.summary: Upload a file or an image to selected environment
put.description: Update file or image content in a row in a Power Platform environment.

parameters.name: content-type
parameters.description: The header describing the content type

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)

parameters.name: x-ms-file-name
parameters.description: Enter a name for the file or image

get.summary: Download a file or an image
get.description: This action allows you to download a file or an image content from a Microsoft Dataverse table. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

parameters.name: Range
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse support chunked downloads

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Enter 'full' for entire image if enabled (default = thumbnail)

schema.description: The content of the file or image

get.summary: Download a file or an image from selected environment
get.description: Retrieve file or image data from a row in a Power Platform environment.

parameters.name: Range
parameters.description: The header parameter required to make Microsoft Dataverse support chunked downloads

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Enter the row's globally unique identifier (GUID)

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Enter 'full' for entire image if enabled (default = thumbnail)

schema.description: The content of the file or image

get.summary: When a flow step is run from a business process flow
get.description: This trigger allows you to start a flow when the Run Flow button in a business process flow step is clicked. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).

post.description: This action allows you to search a Microsoft Dataverse environment using Relevance Search, and returns the rows that match the search term most closely. This connector was formerly known as Common Data Service (current environment).
post.x-ms-description-for-model: Full-text search across one or more Dataverse tables using Relevance Search. Returns rows ranked by relevance score. Unlike list_rows (which uses structured OData filters), this tool performs keyword/text matching across all fields. Use this for keyword searches; use list_rows for structured field-value filtering.

post.description: Provides Remote MCP Server access to Dataverse
Provides Remote MCP Server access to Dataverse with preview tools
(deprecated) MCP server for Sales scenario which helps with Lead Qualification, Outreach Email Drafting and Sending Email to Customer.
(Deprecated) D365 Customer Service MCP server provides tools to solve customer's issues based on information recorded in case(incident) and account entities.
Dynamics 365 ERP MCP with tools

Dataverse MCP Server. This server can be used to get information from Dataverse as well as get knowledge from Dataverse and various other tabular sources. To check what all sources this server can access, use the list_knowledge_sources tool. list_knowledge_sources responsds with the source and tables that this server can access.
(Deprecated) Dynamics 365 Contact Center MCP with tools
Dynamics 365 Conversation Orchestrator MCP with tools (Preview)

@odata.id.description: Enter the row URL using OData ID from a previous step or typing the full URL (eg. https://org0.crm.dynamics.com/api/data/v9.0/faxes(3ce6c728-3c8a-4b55-a4ee-a251b253c3ee)
@odata.id.x-ms-description-for-model: OData URL of the related row. Format: https://<org>.crm.dynamics.com/api/data/v9.0/<entity_plural>(<guid>). The hostname, entity name, and GUID must come from your environment data -- do not guess or use placeholder values.

search.description: Enter a search term, eg. Contoso. Searches modifiers like boolean operators, wildcards, fuzzy search, proximity search etc. require the search type full
search.x-ms-description-for-model: The search term or keywords to find. Matches against all text fields in the target tables. Example: 'Contoso' or 'active policy'.

searchtype.description: Enter whether simple or full search syntax should be used (default is simple)
searchtype.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. Search syntax mode: 'simple' (default) or 'full' (Lucene query syntax).

searchmode.description: Enter whether any or all of the search terms must be matched (default is any)
searchmode.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. 'any' (default): match if any term matches. 'all': all terms must match.

top.description: Enter the number of search results to be listed (default = 50)
top.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. Maximum number of search results to return. Default: 50. Set to a smaller value (e.g., 10) when you only need a few results.

filter.description: Enter an Odata style filter expression to narrow the search
filter.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. OData-style filter to further restrict search results. Example: "statecode eq 0".

entities.description: Enter a comma-separated list of tables to be searched (default is all tables)

items.description: Enter a table unique name to be searched (default is all tables)

entities.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. List of table names to restrict the search to. If omitted, searches all tables. Example: ["contacts", "accounts"].

orderby.description: Enter a comma-separated list of column unique names followed by asc or desc

items.description: Enter a column unique name followed by asc or desc

orderby.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. List of sort expressions. Each is "column_name direction" where direction is 'asc' or 'desc'. Example: ["createdon desc"].

facets.description: Enter a comma-separated list of facet queries to narrow the search

items.description: Enter a facet query to narrow the search

facets.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. Facet fields for grouping results. Example: ["address1_city"].

skip.description: Enter the number of search results to be skipped
skip.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. Number of results to skip for pagination. Default: 0.

returntotalrecordcount.x-ms-description-for-model: Optional. If true, includes total_row_count in the response. Default: false.

totalrecordcount.description: Total count of results (-1 if returntotalrecordcount is set to false)
